texto: Opening day is always so cool to be a part of and to people watch. The Pacific Classic, which I've had the privilege of riding in a couple of times, is great. Every day at Del Mar is fantastic. I love the smells, working out on the beach, running the stairs. It's just a healthy environment. I love it.

texto: So many times, people told me I can't do this or can't do that. My nature is that I don't listen very well. I'm very determined, and I believe in myself. My parents brought me up that way. Thank God for that. I don't let anything stand in my way.

texto: There are other things that I could do, but there's really nothing that I love as much as horse racing.

texto: I'm just so lucky - my office is a racetrack. That is something I'm very thankful for. It's exciting, and it's challenging, and there are a lot of emotions and nerves that come with it, but right there before the gates open, before that minute and a half or two minutes of the race, it just hits you.

texto: I don't even know why, but I've just always done it - I don't walk on handicapped parking spaces. I don't like to step on the blue lines. I always step over them. I don't know what the deal is. I don't know if it's a fear of injury, or a disrespectful thing, or if I just don't want to think about something like that happening.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Chantal Sutherland: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de limites e escolhas conscientes. O trecho “I don't even know why, but I've just always done it - I don't walk on handicapped parking spaces.…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, blue, walk pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de walk e step.
